title: Mexican Rebuild DVDs
wikitext:
[]: Mexican Rebuild DVDÂ´s             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Mon Nov 10, 2014 12:55 am So, I went this weekend at a store here at my city and I ran across this: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Nov 10, 2014 12:07 pm Pretty interesting! Anime is pretty popular in Mexico, no? Remastered Sailor Moon on DVD is getting released there as "Talk Boxes" or something like that, I think. Please review the blurays, if you get them! In terms of international Webuild, I think Poland only has 1.11 officially out, everything else is just fansubs (which sucks, IMO).

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Mon Nov 10, 2014 6:11 pm It seems lately that American localization companies really go out of their way to appease foreign audiences these days. Did you know NetherRealm actually recruited the old DCAU voice actors for their Brazilian audience when they did the Dub for Injustice Gods Among Us? Funimation probably figured they should put as much devotion as they can into their other dubs as their english ones. but Tony Rodríguez (Misato)

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Mon Nov 10, 2014 9:01 pm @SSD: Yup. Anime is popular in Mexico, many were introduced with the popular dubbed anime of the 90's and early 2000's... Sakura Card Captors, Sailor Moon, Dragon Ball, Saint Seiya, Evangelion and many, many more enjoyed unbiased dubbing, that is, it was not "adapted" or censored in any ways... I know that several episodes of Dragon Ball and Sailor Moon got butchered in the US due to censor constrains (don't get me started on "Cardcaptors")... And I don't see me searching for the BR's... Even when that version costs 12 US too... I did some more research and found someone reviewing the Blu Ray versions and they are LAME... They contain basically the same as the DVD: the movie with 3 audios, no TV spots, trailers, making-of featurette nor anything at all... So Ray , no, american companies do not effort to appease foreign audiences... They did not gave the budget for the whole cast, complete extras or even decent boxing... Also, "Toni Rodriguez" is short of "An toni eta Rodriguez"... EDIT: So, curiosity got the best of me and so... I went to buy the 2.22 DVD Besides the fact that as I suspected it has none of the extras... I must say... I simply LOVED the dub of Mari... Both her voice pitch and adaptation of dialogue are amazing... I guess I wont know a thing of Q until Funimations "finishes" their version... But I'll let you know how it turns out...
